Text:
A plot of land is required to build an estate. In addition, the size of the estate must match the size of the plot on which it is built.
■Estate Size and Maximum Number of Furnishings
　・Small: 200 indoor, 20 outdoor
　・Medium: 300 indoor, 30 outdoor
　・Large: 400 indoor, 40 outdoor

A plot of land is required to build an estate. In addition, the size of the estate must match the size of the plot on which it is built.

■Estate Size and Maximum Number of Furnishings
　・Small: 300 indoor, 40 outdoor
　・Medium: 450 indoor, 60 outdoor
　・Large: 600 indoor, 80 outdoor

All types of housing come with a storeroom for keeping one's furnishings. The larger the residence, the bigger the storeroom. The maximum number of furnishings that can be stored is as follows:

　・Private chamber: 100 indoor
　・Apartment: 100 indoor
　・Small: 200 indoor, 20 outdoor
　・Medium: 300 indoor, 30 outdoor
　・Large: 400 indoor, 40 outdoor

Chocobo stables and miniature aetherytes cannot be placed in the storeroom.

Certain items, meanwhile, are stored in stasis. For instance, plants will cease to grow and wheel stands will cease to charge.

An estate hall cannot be removed while items are in storage. In the event that your estate hall is demolished, you may reclaim stored items by speaking with the resident caretaker within 35 days of repossession.

All types of housing come with a storeroom for keeping one's furnishings. The larger the residence, the bigger the storeroom. The maximum number of furnishings that can be stored is as follows:

　・Private chamber: 150 indoor
　・Apartment: 150 indoor
　・Small: 300 indoor, 40 outdoor
　・Medium: 450 indoor, 60 outdoor
　・Large: 600 indoor, 80 outdoor

Chocobo stables and miniature aetherytes cannot be placed in the storeroom.

Certain items, meanwhile, are stored in stasis. For instance, plants will cease to grow and wheel stands will cease to charge.

An estate hall cannot be removed while items are in storage. In the event that your estate hall is demolished, you may reclaim stored items by speaking with the resident caretaker within 35 days of repossession.

Battle High is a status that increases damage dealt and HP restored by healing actions according to a player's knockouts and assists. The maximum Battle High value is 100, and for every increment of 20, the status increases in strength. Being incapacitated will reduce the Battle High value by half.

Battle High is a status that increases damage dealt and HP restored by healing actions according to a player's knockouts and assists. The maximum Battle High value is 100, and for every increment of 20, the status increases in strength. The amount the value increases is determined by the Battle High level of the incapacitated player.

The following settings concerning the camera's placement and focal point can be adjusted:
・Tilt (Tumble)
・Distance (Dolly)
・Positioning (Track)
・Rotation (Roll)
・Lens (Zoom)

■Tilt (Tumble)
This setting affects the camera angle with respect to the focal point (your character). It can be adjusted using the right stick on a gamepad or by left-clicking and dragging the mouse.
※Use of the positioning (track) settings may change the portrait's focal point.

■Distance (Dolly)
Camera distance functions similarly to the zoom function in the overworld. It can be adjusted by pressing  and tilting the right stick on a gamepad, or by scrolling with the mouse wheel while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Positioning (Track)
Positioning moves the focal point both horizontally and vertically, and can be adjusted via the left stick on a gamepad, or by right-clicking and dragging the mouse while the cursor is over your portrait.

■Rotation (Roll)
This slider, found below the portrait currently being edited, rotates the camera itself to adjust the direction of its focus.

■Lens (Zoom)
As opposed to the camera itself, this setting affects the camera lens's zoom to make the field of view wider or narrower. Its slider is also found below the portrait.

■Lottery Land Purchases
In a lottery housing area, you may enter the lottery for a plot of land by accessing the placard located at its entrance and selecting “Enter Lottery.”

■Lottery Schedule
A lottery's entry period will last for five days (Earth time), after which the lottery results will be announced. This results period will last for four days (Earth time). The lottery will be repeated in this way until all eligible plots of land are won and claimed.

■Entry Requirements
Characters that fulfill the requirements listed below are eligible to submit one lottery entry per lottery period. Once submitted, a lottery entry may not be withdrawn.
The requirements for entering the lottery vary depending on whether you are a private buyer or a representative of your free company.

　・Entering as a Private Buyer
　　1. You must have at least one class at level 50.
　　2. You must hold a rank of second lieutenant or above in one of the Grand Companies.

　・Entering as a Free Company Representative
　　1. Your free company must be authorized to acquire land.
　　2. Your free company must have four or more members.
　　3. You must be authorized by your free company to purchase and relinquish land.
　　4. You must have been a member of your free company for 30 days or longer.

**To purchase land in Empyreum, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Litany of Peace,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.**
**To purchase land in Shirogane, you must first complete the main scenario quest “Not without Incident,” whether you are a private buyer or free company representative.**
　※Please note that all requirements listed above must be fulfilled at the time of both entry and purchase.


■Entry Deposits
To enter the lottery for a plot of land, you are required to pay the full sum of its value up front.

■Results
During the results period, you may confirm lottery results for a plot of land by accessing the placard located at its entrance.

　・Losing the Lottery
　　-You will receive a 100% refund of the deposit paid at entry.

　・Winning the Lottery
　　-You will be prompted to confirm the purchase of the plot of land.
　　-If purchasing a new plot, the amount of your deposit will be applied in full to your purchase, and you will receive no refund.
　　-If relocating from an existing plot, in principle, approximately 15% of the value of the old plot will be refunded to you.
　　※Please note that the refunded amount may not exceed the value of the new plot.
　　-You may finalize your purchase of a plot at any time during the results period. Once the results period has ended, however, you will automatically forfeit your claim to that plot.

**　　Should you fail to claim your plot of land, a 50% cancellation fee will be deducted from the deposit made at entry.
**
※Regardless of lottery results, any and all refunds for a given lottery period expire 90 days (Earth time) after the results period.
Please be sure to access the same placard by which you entered the lottery within the allotted time. Failure to do so will result in a loss of any potential refunds.

The requirements for entering the lottery vary depending on whether you are a private buyer or a representative of your free company.

■Entry Deposits
To enter the lottery for a plot of land, you are required to pay the full sum of its value up front.

■Results
During the results period, you may confirm lottery results for a plot of land by accessing the placard located at its entrance.

**　　Should you fail to claim your plot of land, a 50% cancellation fee will be deducted from the deposit made at entry.
**
※Regardless of lottery results, any and all refunds for a given lottery period expire 90 days (Earth time) after the results period.
Please be sure to access the same placard by which you entered the lottery within the allotted time. Failure to do so will result in a loss of any potential refunds.
※Regardless of lottery results, changing your Home World after entering a lottery will result in the forfeiture of any refunds.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in the Palace of the Dead. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

A fixed party is also required to challenge floor 100 and beyond. Moreover, your Party KO count must be zero. Please note that the Party KO count will also increase in the event your party abandons the duty.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in the Palace of the Dead. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

A fixed party is also required to challenge floor 101 and beyond. Please note that should your party KO in the process, your save data will become inaccessible. Abandoning the duty will also count as a party KO.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in Heaven–on–High. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

A fixed party is also required to challenge floor 31 and beyond. Moreover, your Party KO count must be zero. Please note that the Party KO count will also increase in the event your party abandons the duty.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in Heaven–on–High. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

A fixed party is also required to challenge floor 31 and beyond. Please note that should your party KO in the process, your save data will become inaccessible. Abandoning the duty will also count as a party KO.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in Eureka Orthos. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

A fixed party is also required to challenge floor 31 and beyond. Moreover, your Party KO count must be zero. Please note that the Party KO count will also increase in the event your party abandons the duty.

Open the glamour dresser to access the **Glamour Creation** window, browse different gear categories, and select an item from your inventory or Armoury Chest. Gear with condition below 100% cannot be stored.

Placing an item into the glamour dresser will reset its spiritbond to 0%, as well as remove customization including glamours, crests, signatures, materia, and most aetherial bonuses. The item will also be bound to you upon removal.
※Each item stored will cost one glamour prism.
※Dyes applied to the item will not be reset.

Open the glamour dresser to access the **Glamour Creation** window, browse different gear categories, and select an item from your inventory or Armoury Chest. Gear with condition below 100% cannot be stored.

Placing an item into the glamour dresser will reset its spiritbond to 0%, as well as remove customization including glamours, dyes associated with existing glamours, crests, signatures, materia, and most aetherial bonuses. The item will also be bound to you upon removal.
※Each item stored will cost one glamour prism.
※Dyes applied to the item will not be reset.

You can change the color of gear and furnishing items through the use of **dyes**, with one pot of dye consumed for each distinct section of an item.
※Some items allow for two dye slots.

Dyed items can be redyed and undyed any number of times.

You can change the color of gear and furnishing items through the use of **dyes**. As well as single-color varieties, there are also multicolor dyes that provide a choice from a range of hues. With either variety, one pot of dye is consumed for each distinct section of an item.
※Some items allow for two dye slots.

Dyed items can be redyed and undyed any number of times.

There are many different colors of dyes. If you wish to apply the same color to both sections or multiple items, you will need to ensure you have enough dye for each.

Both dyes and terebinth are available from vendors and the market boards, and can also be crafted by Disciples of the Hand from level 30 onwards.

When it comes to dyes, there are single-color varieties, as well as multicolor dyes that provide a choice from a range of hues. With either variety, one pot of dye is consumed for each distinct section of an item. If you wish to apply the same color to both sections or multiple items, you will need to ensure you have enough dye for each.

Both dyes and terebinth are available from vendors and the market boards, and can also be crafted by Disciples of the Hand from level 30 onwards.

Use the colored circles near the top to switch between color categories, then choose your desired dye from the colored squares beneath. Any colors not currently in your possession will be marked with an “X” icon.

You may also use the subcommand menu to set **favored dyes**, enabling you to apply your preferred color scheme to multiple items with ease.

Confirm your chosen color settings in the preview pane, then select **Dye** to begin the process.

Use the colored circles near the top to switch between color categories, then choose your desired hue from the colored squares beneath. Any colors for which you do not possess the required dye will be marked with an “X” icon.

You may also use the subcommand menu to set **favored dyes**, enabling you to apply your preferred color scheme to multiple items with ease.

Confirm your chosen color settings in the preview pane, then select **Dye** to begin the process.

Phantom actions can be executed and toggled using the following methods:

■Keyboard and Mouse
By clicking phantom action icons displayed on the HUD.

■Controller
By pressing  with the cross hotbar active.
＋: Select Phantom Action
＋: Execute Phantom Action

■Registering Duty Actions to the Hotbar / Cross Hotbar
If you register Duty Actions I and II found under the Duties tab in Actions & Traits to your hotbar or cross hotbar, these icons will automatically switch to Execute Phantom Action and Toggle Phantom Action, respectively, while on the Occult Crescent.

■Registering Phantom Actions to the Hotbar / Cross Hotbar
If you register Phantom Actions I-V found under the Duties tab in Actions & Traits to your hotbar or cross hotbar, these icons will automatically switch to their respective phantom actions while on the Occult Crescent.

By clicking phantom action icons displayed on the HUD.

Your Party KO count must be zero to challenge areas beyond traverse stone 31. Please note that the Party KO count will also increase in the event your party abandons the duty.

It is also possible to receive a score for your performance in Pilgrim's Traverse. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

It is possible to receive a score for your performance in Pilgrim's Traverse. In order to do so, you must enter with a fixed party.

Please note that should your party KO while challenging areas marked by stones 31 and beyond, your save data will become inaccessible. Abandoning the duty will also count as a party KO.

■Multiple Object Selection
When using a mouse, hold Ctrl and left-click the icons you wish to select together.
When using a controller, press  to select multiple objects.

■Multiple Object Selection
When using a mouse, hold Ctrl and left-click the icons you wish to select together.
When using a , press  to select multiple objects.
※Multiple objects cannot be selected when real-time sharing.

Real-time sharing allows you to modify a strategy board together with party members.

While all players will be able to view the strategy board, only the player who initiated real-time sharing can make direct edits.

Please note that real-time sharing will be suspended during combat or when players leave or join your party. However, any changes made to the strategy board will be saved automatically to the Shared List, where you can resume editing at a later time.

Because real-time edits are overwritten periodically, you may wish to create a copy to preserve the original strategy board.

Real-time sharing allows you to modify a strategy board together with party members.

While all players will be able to view the strategy board, only the player who initiated real-time sharing can make direct edits.
Moreover, the Undo and Redo functions, as well as the selection of multiple objects, are not available when real-time sharing.

Please note that real-time sharing will be suspended during combat or when players leave or join your party. However, any changes made to the strategy board will be saved automatically to the Shared List, where you can resume editing at a later time.

Because real-time edits are overwritten periodically, you may wish to create a copy to preserve the original strategy board.

■Reset Conditions
The following actions will break a gold count streak:
・Initiating a class B, C, or D mission.
・Completing a mission with a rating below Gold Star.
・Abandoning a mission (unless the mission was automatically abandoned due to your mech ops pilot application having been selected).

Conversely, the following actions will not break a gold count streak:
・Taking part in mech ops.
・Taking part in cosmic exploration projects.
・Initiating and/or completing critical missions.

■Reset Conditions
The following actions will break a gold count streak:
・Initiating a class B, C, or D mission.
・Completing a mission with a rating below Gold Star.
・Abandoning a mission (unless the mission was automatically abandoned due to your mech ops pilot application having been selected).

Conversely, the following actions will not break a gold count streak:
・Taking part in mech ops.
・Taking part in cosmic exploration projects.
・Initiating and/or completing other types of missions, such as critical missions.

The icon representing saved outfits will also indicate the number of individual items presently being used as part of the outfit glamour. An orange number indicates all parts have been stored together.

As with standard glamours, outfit glamours will appear in each tab of the glamour dresser. Their item descriptions will also indicate they are part of an outfit glamour. It is worth noting that, when removing outfit glamours from the glamour dresser, they cannot be removed as individual pieces.

The icon representing saved outfits will also indicate the number of individual items presently being used as part of the outfit glamour. An orange number indicates all parts have been stored together.

As with standard glamours, outfit glamours will appear in each tab of the glamour dresser. Their item descriptions will also indicate they are part of an outfit glamour.
